name: Peer Ki Gali (Mughal Road)
description: The highest point on the historic Mughal Road (3,485m). It is renowned for its sweeping, majestic views of the Pir Panjal mountain ranges and deep valleys.
history: Named after a famous Hindu saint (Peer) Sheikh Ahmed Karim. The Mughal Road itself was historically used by emperors Akbar, Jahangir, and Shah Jahan to conquer Kashmir.
famousFor: Historic Mughal route, stunning Pir Panjal views, Shrine of the Peer.
